dsl_rule.translation_notes: Strangle write strategy: Entry when price is between lower strike (60) and upper strike (70). Exit condition uses upside breakeven (77 = 70 call strike + 7 points premium). The signal specifies holding to expiration where profit occurs if price remains between strikes. Actual strike prices (70 call, 60 put) and premiums (4 and 3 = 7 total) are author-silent, so conditions use the concrete example values from the source text. Exit at upside breakeven represents a defined risk management point; downside exit would trigger at 53 breakeven (60 - 7) but configured as primary exit for simplicity.

backtest_rule.reason: Strangle write strategy: Entry when price is between lower strike (60) and upper strike (70). Exit condition uses upside breakeven (77 = 70 call strike + 7 points premium). The signal specifies holding to expiration where profit occurs if price remains between strikes. Actual strike prices (70 call, 60 put) and premiums (4 and 3 = 7 total) are author-silent, so conditions use the concrete example values from the source text. Exit at upside breakeven represents a defined risk management point; downside exit would trigger at 53 breakeven (60 - 7) but configured as primary exit for simplicity.

source_rule_text: Sell a strangle (sell out-of-the-money call and out-of-the-money put) when underlying is between the two strike prices to profit from time decay if price stays between strikes at expiration.
